mnemonics.title: I B H I S - Induction Proof Steps
mnemonics.content:
I - Identify the statement P(n)
B - Base case (prove for n=1)
H - Hypothesis (assume P(k) is true)
I - Inductive step (prove P(k) → P(k+1))
S - State conclusion (P(n) true for all n)

mnemonics.title: BASE as Foundation
mnemonics.content:
B - Begin with smallest value
A - Assert the base case
S - Show it holds
E - Establish the chain

mnemonics.title: Strong uses ALL Previous
mnemonics.content:
Weak: Just the Previous (k)
Strong: ALL Previous (1 to k)
Think of it like building a tower - strong induction uses all lower floors to build the next one.

acronyms.usage: The statement to be proved about natural number n
The assumption that P(k) is true used to prove P(k+1)
The initial proof that P(1) or P(0) is true
Every non-empty set of naturals has a least element

visualTips.visualization: Imagine an infinite line of dominoes. The base case tips the first domino. The inductive step ensures each domino tips the next. Once the first falls, ALL will fall - this is mathematical induction.

Think of induction as climbing an infinite ladder. The base case puts the first rung within reach. The inductive step proves you can reach each rung from the previous one. Therefore, you can climb the entire ladder.
Weak: Step on stone k to reach k+1. Strong: Use ALL stones from start to k to reach k+1. Both get you to the top, but strong uses more help.
Think of induction as building a pyramid from the bottom up. The base case is the foundation - without it, the entire structure collapses.

analogies.analogy: Like a chain where each link is connected to the next. The base case is the first link forged, and the inductive step is the process that connects every subsequent link.
Like Russian nesting dolls - each doll contains a smaller version of itself until you reach the smallest (base case).

Proving recursive algorithm correctness by induction is like proving a recipe works: if you follow the base instructions correctly and each step transforms the previous result correctly, the final dish will be correct.
